persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypothesis: the bio is a two-initial 'W. R.' form, which on its own would be weak, and an honoured retiree can stay listed after retirement. This fails on every count: there is exactly ONE Kynsey official and ONE Kynsey biography in the entire corpus, so no same-name relative competes; the position is the unique single-holder office 'Principal Civil Medical Officer and Inspector-General of Hospitals' matching verbatim; the dates align (bio appointed 1875, retired 1897 / staff list 1878-1896); and the C.M.G. honour is shared (bio 'C.M.G. 1888', staff list shows 'C.M.G.' in 1894 and 1896 -- the deterministic honour check missed this). The staff list also spells out 'William R. Kynsey', matching the bio forename.

evidence_cited.fact: W. R. Kynsey | Principal Civil Medical Officer and Inspector-General of Hospitals
William R. Kynsey | Principal Civil Medical Officer and Inspector-General of Hospitals | C.M.G.

persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second Kynsey holding the same Ceylon medical post, or a chronology conflict with the 1875-1897 tenure.

notes: Unique surname in both staff lists and biographies, unique single-holder office matching verbatim, and a shared C.M.G. honour that the mechanical honour check failed to detect. Publish-grade.
